title: An Old English Miscellany (Morris 1872 edited composite)
summary: The General Corpus artifact copies University of Michigan electronic item AHA6129.0001.001, a selective 2006 OCR encoding of Richard Morris's 1872 EETS edition. Morris's edition is an edition-level composite: one Bestiary, five Kentish sermons, and thirty-one numbered miscellanies, with parallel versions nested under their count-once works. The source chain spans multiple manuscript witnesses, earlier printed conduits for the Trinity Proverbs version, abstract Latin and French source traditions, and distinct digital carriers. A combined Trinity host is separated from component B.14.39; selected-image Bodleian objects are not mistaken for complete facsimiles; the 1857 CUL catalog contains the complete Ten Commandments target but is not proved to be Morris's direct base.
review_notes: The selected intellectual language is Middle English. Latin and French occur only in source traditions, an omitted comparator appendix, rubrics, or editorial paratext and therefore are not added as selected-work languages.
Thirty-seven count-once intellectual items remain distinct from thirty-six outer XML divisions, forty-six work-level streams, fifty-eight component-level streams, and raw XML line/group totals.
Every entity has a separate access record and a separate rights record; physical-text rights do not silently cover manuscript photography or provider terms.
Distinct facsimile carriers are separate entities. Bodleian Laud and Douce public manifests are selected-image objects with the target absent.
The complete Trinity bundle is a facsimile of combined host B.14.39-40; target component B.14.39 is related through contains, not silently equated with the host.
The Coxe-Hunt 1973 catalog expression is separate from its ORA PDF delivery carrier; CC0 is attached only to the exact ORA digital-object layer.

The 1857 CUL catalog expression is separate from its Internet Archive PDF scan carrier; public-domain status of the historical expression does not automatically license modern scan, OCR, or metadata layers.

entities.label: General Corpus repository XML artifact aha6129
entities.description: The repository copy of the U-M/CME encoding, fixed to the reviewed Git commit and modeled separately from the U-M digital encoding and Morris's print edition.
notes: The reviewed file is 362,406 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic item AHA6129.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 OCR-derived electronic transcription of selected parts of Morris's edition. The U-M header says no corrections or content editing were undertaken and identifies TEI Libraries level 1 encoding with linked page images.
notes: The electronic selection includes the title page, printed pp. 1-200 and 209-232; it omits front matter pp. v-xv, Latin Appendix I pp. 201-208, and back matter pp. 233-308.

entities.label: Richard Morris, An Old English Miscellany (1872)
entities.description: EETS Original Series 49. Morris edits one Bestiary, five Kentish sermons, thirty-one numbered miscellanies, a Latin source/comparator appendix, and two further metrical versions of The XI Pains of Hell.

bibliographic.citation: An Old English Miscellany, Containing a Bestiary, Kentish Sermons, Proverbs of Alfred, Religious Poems of the Thirteenth Century, ed. Richard Morris, EETS Original Series 49 (London: N. Trübner and Co., 1872).
bibliographic.title: An Old English Miscellany
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 49
bibliographic.pages: Selected electronic text: title page, 1-200, 209-232; complete volume additionally includes preliminary and back matter and Appendix I, 201-208

notes: The edition title's “Old English” is historical wording; the selected medieval texts are cataloged as Middle English.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Morris's 1872 miscellany
entities.description: Complete PDF scan and item metadata for the Morris edition, cached as separate files.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF scan with associated Internet Archive metadata

entities.label: Wright and Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, volume I (1845)
entities.description: A printed source for a Trinity B.14.39 text of the Proverbs of Alfred at pp. 170-188, with Jesus/Oxford comparison supplied through Frederic Madden.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. I (London: John Russell Smith, 1845), 170-188.
bibliographic.title: Reliquiae Antiquae

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Reliquiae Antiquae, volume I (1845)
Kemble, The Dialogue of Salomon and Saturnus (1848)
entities.description: A printed Trinity B.14.39 Proverbs text at pp. 225-248, consulted as a supporting source for Morris's second Proverbs version.

bibliographic.citation: John M. Kemble, The Dialogue of Salomon and Saturnus (London: Ælfric Society, 1848), 225-248.
bibliographic.title: The Dialogue of Salomon and Saturnus

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Kemble's Salomon and Saturnus (1848)
British Library, Arundel MS 292
entities.description: The manuscript witness used by Morris for the verse Bestiary at ff. 4r-10v. The following work starts at f. 11. Morris associates the source with Theobaldus, while the current BL description chiefly attributes the related Latin content to Hildebertus; this conflict is retained.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, Laud Misc. 471
entities.description: The manuscript witness for the five Kentish sermons, collectively at ff. 128v-133v. The public IIIF manifest is a selected-image object and does not include the target locus.

entities.label: Bodleian selected-image IIIF object for Laud Misc. 471
entities.description: A selected-image manifest containing only f. 65r. It is not a complete manuscript facsimile and the Kentish-sermon target at ff. 128v-133v is absent.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest for selected image

entities.label: Coxe and Hunt catalogue of the Laudian manuscripts (1973 reissue)
entities.description: Catalog description for Laud Misc. 471. It gives the five English sermons at f. 128v and the following Latin/French material beginning at f. 133v, but does not contain the target sermon text beyond description and incipit.

bibliographic.citation: H. O. Coxe, Laudian Manuscripts, reprinted with corrections and additions by R. W. Hunt (Oxford: Bodleian Library, 1973), item 18, printed pp. 343-344.
bibliographic.title: Laudian Manuscripts

entities.label: Bodleian Library, Jesus College MS 29
entities.description: Principal manuscript source for Miscellanies I-XIX and the Jesus versions paired with Cotton for XX-XXV. Morris's historical foliation and the current foliation are not related by one blanket offset.

holding.notes: Target spans current ff. 144r-155r, 169r-195r, and 198r-200v; exact item-level mappings are recorded in work metadata.

entities.label: Complete Bodleian IIIF facsimile of Jesus College MS 29
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 532 JPEG derivatives at 1800-pixel width

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, combined host B.14.39-40
entities.description: The current combined catalog and digital host containing manuscript components B.14.39 and B.14.40. It is modeled separately from the target component B.14.39.

holding.notes: The combined description reports B.14.39 as 87 folios and B.14.40 as 93 folios.

physical_description.format: Two manuscript components represented in one current host record

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, B.14.39
entities.description: The target manuscript component within combined host B.14.39-40. The Proverbs of Alfred occupies ff. 85r-87v, combined-host canvases 173-178; f. 85r begins “At Siforde,” and the poem ends on f. 87v before Latin text.

holding.notes: Recovered after an interval described in the current catalog as 1863-1896; not presently lost.

physical_description.extent: 87 folios in the combined catalog description

entities.label: Complete Trinity IIIF facsimile of combined host B.14.39-40
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 372 JPEG derivatives at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A.ix
entities.description: Witness for the Cotton versions of Miscellanies XX-XXV at ff. 246r-249r and for Miscellany XXVI, Will and Wit, at f. 246v. The paired-text target for XX-XXV corresponds to IIIF canvases 501-507.

entities.label: Complete British Library IIIF facsimile of Cotton Caligula A.ix
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 543 JPEG derivatives at 1200-pixel width

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 27909A
entities.description: Source witness for Miscellany XXVII, A Prayer to Our Lady, at f. 2. No public digital surrogate was verified.

entities.label: British Library, Egerton MS 613
entities.description: Source witness for Miscellanies XXVIII-XXX: XXX at f. 1v, XXVIII and the start of XXIX at f. 2r, and the end of XXIX at f. 2v.

entities.label: British Library IIIF facsimile of Egerton MS 613
entities.description: A publicly viewable 197-canvas IIIF object. It was inspected live but not cached because British Library terms prohibit database construction or recirculation absent permission.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.6.15
entities.description: Witness for Miscellany XXXI, The Ten Commandments, at f. 21r-v. Morris dates it before 1300, while the 1857 catalog assigns an early-fifteenth-century date; the conflict remains unresolved.

holding.notes: Current item-level online catalog record not established; enquiries go to specialcollections@lib.cam.ac.uk.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library manuscripts catalogue, volume II (1857)
entities.description: Historical catalog description of Ff.6.15. Its entry at print pp. 521-522 includes the complete ten-line Ten Commandments text on p. 522. It is supporting source evidence, not proof that Morris directly used the catalog or manuscript.

bibliographic.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. II (Cambridge: University Press, 1857), 521-522.
bibliographic.title: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 3093
entities.description: Base manuscript identified by Morris for Appendix I's Latin source/comparator material at ff. 36r-38r.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 10415
entities.description: Collation source named by Morris for Appendix I. Exact target folios were not established.

entities.label: British Library, Arundel MS 243
entities.description: A collation source named by Morris for Appendix I. The relevant cataloged text begins at f. 364 and precedes the next item at f. 384, but the exact comparator endpoint was not established.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, Douce 302
entities.description: Witness for Morris's Appendix II version of The XI Pains of Hell at ff. 17r-18v. The public selected-image manifest omits this target locus.

entities.label: Bodleian selected-image IIIF object for Douce 302
entities.description: A selected-image manifest containing a spread at ff. 22v-23r and f. 27v. It is not a complete facsimile, and the target at ff. 17r-18v is absent.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest for selected images

entities.label: Bodleian Library, Vernon manuscript
entities.description: Witness for Morris's Appendix III version of The XI Pains of Hell at ff. 230r-231r, IIIF canvases 347-349.

entities.label: Complete Bodleian IIIF facsimile of the Vernon manuscript
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 710 JPEG derivatives at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Latin Physiologus and Theobaldus bestiary source tradition
entities.description: An abstract catalog-level source tradition used to model Morris's claim that the Middle English Bestiary adapts Latin Physiologus/Theobaldus materials. It is not a physical manuscript, a direct exemplar, or an assertion that every chapter has one identical source.
notes: No physical exemplar is attached to this entity.

entities.label: Maurice de Sully French sermon source tradition
entities.description: An abstract catalog-level source tradition for the French sermon materials adapted into the five Middle English Kentish sermons. It is not a physical exemplar and does not make the selected Middle English text bilingual.
notes: No physical exemplar is attached to this entity.

entities.label: Oxford Research Archive PDF delivery of Coxe and Hunt's Laudian Manuscripts
entities.description: The digital delivery carrier for the 1973 catalog expression. The complete PDF contains catalog description and incipit only, not the selected Kentish-sermon text.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Cambridge manuscripts catalogue, volume II (1857)
entities.description: The complete PDF scan carrier for the 1857 catalog expression. Its Ff.6.15 entry contains the complete ten-line Ten Commandments target.

scope.notes: Preliminary matter, Latin Appendix I pp. 201-208, and back matter pp. 233-308 are omitted.

access.access_method: Consult repository catalog and request reading-room or reproduction access.
notes: Ask the repository first whether an existing complete surrogate of the current physical host can be supplied.

If none exists, request full-color imaging of the entire current host, including covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, endleaves, blanks, stubs, and every recto and verso; record the exact target locus separately.
Use a partial target-locus order only if complete-host imaging is infeasible. Access or delivery does not imply permission to quote, approval of this cataloging, or permission to reuse images.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete Morris volume with preliminary matter, main texts, appendices, notes, glossary, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Title page, pp. 1-200, and pp. 209-232 only
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding selectively represents the title page and Middle English text streams, omitting front matter pp. v-xv, Latin Appendix I pp. 201-208, and back matter pp. 233-308. The header says no corrections or content editing were undertaken.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple manuscript versions of Proverbs of Alfred, Miscellanies XX-XXV, The XI Pains of Hell, and Appendix I source material
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel text columns or adjacent version streams, followed by separately labeled appendices
editorial_practices.description: Morris prints and collates multiple versions without thereby creating additional count-once intellectual works. Appendices II-III are explicitly two more metrical versions of Miscellany XIX.
editorial_practices.scope: Miscellany XV, XIX-XXV, and Appendix I

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, Laud Misc. 471.
evidence.summary: Current catalog evidence for the Kentish sermons at ff. 128v-133v and the following source-language material.

evidence.citation: Bodleian selected-image IIIF manifest for Laud Misc. 471.
evidence.summary: Selected-image manifest containing only f. 65r; confirms target absence and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: H. O. Coxe, Laudian Manuscripts, corrected by R. W. Hunt (1973), complete ORA PDF.
evidence.summary: The complete ORA PDF contains catalog description and incipit only, not the target sermon text. ORA's CC0 assertion is scoped to the exact deposited digital object and is not generalized to the separately modeled 1973 catalog expression.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, Jesus College MS 29.
evidence.summary: Current catalog evidence for manuscript identity and foliation.

evidence.citation: Bodleian complete IIIF manifest for Jesus College MS 29.
evidence.summary: Complete cached IIIF bundle and direct inspection evidence for target loci, current foliation, and CC BY 4.0 licensing.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge manuscripts catalogue, B.14.39-40.
evidence.summary: Current combined-host description, component extents, recovery history, and target component identity.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge complete IIIF manifest for B.14.39-40.
evidence.summary: Complete cached combined-host bundle, target-locus inspection, and CC BY-NC 4.0 rights evidence.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A.ix.
evidence.summary: Current holding and manuscript-description evidence for the Cotton versions of Miscellanies XX-XXVI.

evidence.citation: British Library complete IIIF manifest for Cotton Caligula A.ix.
evidence.locator: 543 canvases; Miscellanies XX-XXV at ff. 246r-249r and Miscellany XXVI at f. 246v; target canvases 501-507
evidence.summary: Complete cached IIIF bundle and target-locus inspection; manifest rights language says public domain in most countries other than the UK, subject to provider terms.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 27909A.
evidence.summary: Current holding and catalog evidence for the Miscellany XXVII Prayer to Our Lady witness at f. 2.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 613.
evidence.summary: Current holding and manuscript-description evidence for Miscellanies XXVIII-XXX.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF manifest for Egerton MS 613.
evidence.summary: Live direct-inspection evidence for target loci and manifest rights language; no local copy was made because provider terms restrict recirculation.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Archives and Modern and Medieval Manuscripts access and search page.
evidence.summary: Current general discovery and enquiry route for Ff.6.15; specialcollections@lib.cam.ac.uk is the contact used when no item-level public record is exposed.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. II (1857), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: print pp. 521-522 / PDF pp. 533-534; complete target text on print p. 522
evidence.summary: Historical description of Ff.6.15 and complete ten-line Ten Commandments text; target is present. The 1857 expression and modern Internet Archive scan carrier are modeled separately, and no item-level scan license was verified.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 3093.
evidence.summary: Current holding evidence for Appendix I's base witness at ff. 36r-38r.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 10415.
evidence.summary: Current holding evidence for an Appendix I collation source; exact target folios remain unresolved.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Arundel MS 243.
evidence.summary: Current holding evidence for an Appendix I comparator beginning at f. 364 before the next item at f. 384; the exact target endpoint remains unresolved.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, Douce 302.
evidence.summary: Current catalog evidence for the Appendix II target at ff. 17r-18v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian selected-image IIIF manifest for Douce 302.
evidence.summary: Selected-image manifest covering ff. 22v-23r and f. 27v; confirms target absence and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, Vernon manuscript.
evidence.summary: Current catalog evidence for the Appendix III target at ff. 230r-231r.

evidence.citation: Bodleian complete IIIF manifest for the Vernon manuscript.
evidence.summary: Complete cached IIIF bundle, target-locus inspection, and CC BY-NC 4.0 rights evidence.

evidence.citation: British Library Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Provider conditions applied to BL images; reviewed terms prohibit database construction or recirculation absent permission, so Egerton derivatives were not cached.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Supports public-domain-in-the-United-States determinations for works published in 1872, 1845, 1848, and 1857.

evidence.citation: Morris, An Old English Miscellany (1872), source statements for the Bestiary and Kentish sermons.
evidence.locator: introductory and source notes for pp. 1-36
evidence.summary: Evidence for scoped Latin Physiologus/Theobaldus and Maurice de Sully French-sermon source traditions, without identifying a direct physical exemplar.

evidence.citation: Target-scoped integrity and inventory audit of source-cache/aha6129.
evidence.locator: access[].local_copies: 14 cached objects; hashes, byte extents, ZIP CRC, manifest-to-inventory mappings, image counts, image SHA-256 and byte checks
evidence.summary: Confirms exact local fixity and complete-bundle integrity for 14 cached objects, including 532 Jesus, 543 Cotton, 710 Vernon, and 372 Trinity image derivatives.

open_questions.question: Can existing complete current-host color surrogates be obtained for the witnesses whose public imaging is absent or selected-only?
next_steps: Ask each repository first for an existing complete current-host surrogate.

If none exists, request full-color imaging of the entire current physical host, including covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, endleaves, blanks, stubs, and every recto and verso; record the target locus separately.
Use partial target-locus imaging only if full-host imaging is infeasible, and keep access, quotation permission, approval, and image reuse as separate questions.

open_questions.question: Did Morris directly consult CUL Ff.6.15, copy the complete 1857 catalog transcription, or use another conduit for Miscellany XXXI?
next_steps: Search Morris's correspondence and editorial papers; until then retain tentative consulted alternatives for the 1857 catalog and physical witness rather than asserting citation or direct transcription.

open_questions.question: How should Morris's Theobaldus characterization of the Bestiary source be reconciled with the current BL description's chiefly Hildebertus attribution?
next_steps: Compare Arundel 292's relevant Latin and English texts with current specialist cataloging while keeping abstract work tradition, manuscript contents, and direct exemplar identity separate.

open_questions.question: What are the exact target loci in Add MS 10415 and Arundel MS 243 for Appendix I, including the exact endpoint of the Arundel 243 comparator passage?
next_steps: Inspect complete host images or request repository folio verification before adding precise Appendix I locators.
